Hawkins General Hawkins, Indiana

56 pieces · five aisles · Hawkins, Indiana, 1983–86 · updated July 2026

Stock up before the lights flicker

Stranger Things, bought carefully. The licensed goods, the campaign table, the Byers living room, and the rest of Hawkins retail: waffles, walkie-talkies, tape decks, denim. Every link goes straight to the retailer.

Snapshot A · Hawkins Snapshot B · Hawkins Snapshot C · Hawkins Snapshot D · Hawkins

Shop the aisles

Aisle I Starcourt 21 pieces Aisle II The Wheeler Basement 8 pieces Aisle III The Byers Living Room 8 pieces Aisle IV The AV Club 8 pieces Aisle V The Five-and-Dime 11 pieces

Aisle II

The Wheeler Basement

Ten hours into the campaign. Dice, handbooks, and a channel the adults never check.

BCA

Bc Link 2.0 Group Communication System

Channel six, code red. Over means over.

Aisle III

The Byers Living Room

String lights, a phone with a cord, waffles for whoever turns up. The wall knows more than it says.

“I am on a curiosity voyage, and I need my paddles to travel.”
D. Henderson, Hawkins Middle AV Club
Aisle IV

The AV Club

Tape decks, headphones, instant film. If it takes batteries, it belongs here.

Over the ears, over the world. The safest place in Hawkins.

The Five-and-Dime

Ringer tees, denim, tube socks. Dress for a town where nothing ever happens.

The record

Stranger Things™ and Netflix are trademarks of their respective owners. Aisle I is the licensed goods. Aisles II through V are the town around them, not licensed product.

Prices and stock come from Channel3 and flicker like everything else in Hawkins; confirm at the retailer.

The arrangement

Links are monetized. When you buy through one, this site earns a commission. Friends don't lie.
